articles.title: OPENAI APPROACHED CURSOR BEFORE WINDSURF
articles.summary: OpenAI held talks with Anysphere, maker of the popular Cursor AI coding tool, before entering discussions to buy rival Windsurf for $3 billion. While acquisition talks with Cursor stalled, OpenAI met with more than 20 companies in the AI coding space before pursuing Windsurf.

articles.title: FIGMA FILES FOR IPO DESPITE MARKET FEARS
articles.summary: Design software maker Figma has confidentially filed IPO paperwork, bucking the trend of tech companies delaying public offerings due to market volatility. Last valued at $12.5 billion, Figma continued to grow after Adobe's $20 billion acquisition was blocked by regulators in 2023.

articles.title: AMERICAN DISRUPTION
articles.summary: Globalization and scale have permanently shifted key production - especially semiconductors - to Asia. Instead of blanket tariffs or reviving low-end manufacturing, the U.S. should prioritize strategic control of advanced technology and components, using its market power to build a resilient, forward-looking supply chain.

articles.title: YOU'RE PROBABLY DOING USER RESEARCH WRONG
articles.summary: Product teams waste resources asking the wrong questions. At a focus group in Venice, users claimed they'd never use Tinder again—yet moments later, nearly everyone admitted meeting someone through the app that year. This contradiction reveals three types of research questions with dramatically different reliability: "What did you do?" questions about past behavior yield trustworthy insights because people accurately remember their actions. "What are you doing?" questions capture unfiltered reactions in real-time, revealing genuine confusion or delight without mental editing. "What would you do?" questions about future behavior are the least reliable, since human imagination is flawed and influenced by virtue signaling, yet most common.

articles.title: YOU SHOULD PROBABLY FORM A MONETIZATION COUNCIL
articles.summary: Most companies have no one truly owning pricing decisions - leading to endless meetings where nothing happens. With AI changing how customers use products, flat-rate pricing won't work when one user consumes 100x more than another. Create a cross-functional team (Sales, Marketing, Product, and Finance) with actual decision-makers who meet regularly to approve experiments and make data-driven changes. Without this process, your pricing will grow stale as your product evolves, leaving money on the table.

articles.title: GPT 4.1 PROMPTING GUIDE
articles.summary: This guide gives some helpful high-level tips and includes a very clear structure for prompts. According to OpenAI developers on X, the prompts in the guide mirror the formatting of the company's training dataset. This means that if you structure your prompts according to this guide, they are more likely to be in-distribution.

articles.title: SHERLOQ (TOOLS)

articles.title: 7 SMART PHRASES THAT MAKE GIVING FEEDBACK EASIER
YOUR BELIEFS ARE YOUR STRATEGY
articles.summary: Every founder should write a manifesto. It helps you get clear about what you're building and why it matters. A good manifesto starts with your customers. What are they trying to do? What stands in their way? Then explain how your approach is different. Why did you build things this way? Most importantly, write down what you believe. These beliefs drive your choices about features, markets, and everything else. They're your real strategy. When you finish writing, you'll see you weren't just making words. You were making choices that help you stand out when talking to customers or investors.

articles.title: ANNOUNCING BASE POWER'S SERIES B FUNDRAISE
articles.summary: Base has secured a $200M Series B funding round to develop a distributed energy network to address grid stability and rising energy demand. The company plans to expand production with a new factory in Texas and enhance utility partnerships nationwide.

articles.title: DON'T SAY "WE HAVE NO COMPETITION"
articles.summary: The myth of the "unique startup" is dangerous—competition exists in every market, though often as the status quo rather than direct rivals.

articles.title: VIBE CHECK: O3 IS HERE—AND IT'S GREAT
articles.summary: OpenAI just launched o3, a new reasoning model, and it feels like the jet engine has finally found its airplane.

articles.title: WHAT IS VIBE CODING? AND IS IT WORTH THE HYPE?
articles.summary: Vibe coding leverages AI to build software by describing tasks and refining generated outcomes, allowing rapid iterations without traditional planning.
